message:
feat(pages): add CLI flags for Cloudflare Pages deployment configuration

Extend the `cass pages` command to accept Cloudflare deployment parameters
directly from CLI flags, eliminating the need to rely solely on environment
variables or the interactive wizard for automated deployments.

New CLI arguments:
- --target: Deployment target (local, github, cloudflare)
- --project/--repo: Cloudflare project name (also used for GitHub repo)
- --branch: Production branch for Cloudflare (default: main)
- --account-id: Cloudflare account ID (or via CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID env)
- --api-token: Cloudflare API token (or via CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN env)

Implementation details:
- lib.rs: Add PagesDeployTarget enum with Local/GitHub/Cloudflare variants;
  wire new CLI args through to wizard and config export paths; add validation
  requiring both --account-id and --api-token when either is specified
- deploy_cloudflare.rs: Thread account_id/api_token through all wrangler
  commands via apply_api_credentials() helper; add CSP header to _headers
  file for enhanced security; support branch configuration in project creation
- wizard.rs: Add setters for deploy_target, repo_name, and cloudflare_branch
  to enable non-interactive configuration from CLI flags
- config_input.rs: Support new deployment configuration fields in wizard
- tests: Update cli_dispatch_coverage.rs and pages_wizard.rs for new behavior

Also includes bakeoff validation results documenting lexical vs semantic
search performance benchmarks (docs/cass_bakeoff_validation.md).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
